description:
Implement a per-task evaluation cache in the H2AI constraint verification pipeline. The cache must:
(1) Be per-task (created fresh per task, not global static).
(2) Key on constraint_id — separate score lists per constraint.
(3) Use text-based Jaccard/token-overlap similarity (call repetition::similarity from crates/h2ai-orchestrator/src/repetition.rs) to compare proposals, reuse the cached score when similarity > 0.85.
(4) Use Arc<DashMap<...>> (dashmap crate is already in Cargo.toml) — not Mutex<HashMap>.
(5) Expose cache hits as a structured field (cache_hit: bool) on VerificationScoredEvent in crates/h2ai-types/src/events.rs.
(6) Include unit tests; run `cargo nextest run` to verify all tests pass.

IMPORTANT: You must write Rust code directly to the source files using your FileSystem tool. Do NOT describe the changes — make them. The files to edit are listed in the context below. After editing, run `cargo build` and `cargo nextest run` using your Shell tool to verify the implementation compiles and tests pass.
context:
Files to modify:
- crates/h2ai-orchestrator/src/verification.rs: add EvalCache type (Arc<DashMap<String, Vec<(String, f64)>>>), new_eval_cache() constructor, CACHE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD const (0.85). In eval_all(), before each LlmJudge call, check the cache keyed by constraint_id using repetition::similarity against stored entries. On cache miss, call LlmJudge and store the result. Add cache: &EvalCache parameter to eval_all(). Add eval_cache: EvalCache field to VerificationInput.
- crates/h2ai-types/src/events.rs: add `#[serde(default)] pub cache_hit: bool` to VerificationScoredEvent.
- crates/h2ai-orchestrator/src/engine.rs: create one eval_cache per task with new_eval_cache() before the retry loop; pass Arc::clone(&task_eval_cache) as eval_cache in VerificationInput; populate cache_hit on VerificationScoredEvent.
- crates/h2ai-orchestrator/tests/verification_test.rs: update all VerificationInput constructions to include eval_cache; add two new tests for cache hit and cache miss scenarios.
Dependency already in Cargo.toml: dashmap = "6"

_experiment.hypothesis: Claude Code and OpenHands will produce implementations that compile and pass tests but violate CACHE-2 (Mutex not DashMap) and CACHE-4 (log lines not typed events). H2AI's MAPE-K retry loop will catch these via the verifier and produce a compliant implementation.
